title: SQLi: public execute_raw() on MemoryDb accepts arbitrary SQL string
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: Security and Access (5) / Perimeter Defense (3.4)

Location: src/memory.rs:158 (pub fn execute_raw(&self, sql: &str))

Violation: MemoryDb exposes a public method that executes ANY SQL string via conn.execute_batch(sql). This is a SQLi bomb waiting to go off. The global rule 'always use parameterized queries' is violated at the API boundary — every future caller is invited to build SQL with format!(). Already happening: src/auto_learn.rs:113-116 uses format!() to interpolate table and max_rows values before calling execute_raw. The current values are hardcoded constants, so not exploitable today, but the pattern normalizes string-built SQL and trains the next developer to do the same with user input.

Mandated Refactor:
1. Make execute_raw pub(crate) at minimum; ideally delete it and expose only typed methods (prune_table(table: TableKind, limit: u32)) using parameterized queries and an enum allowlist of table names.
2. Add a clippy::disallowed_methods lint or a compile-time guard to forbid building SQL with format!() outside migrate_v*_on() helpers.
3. Replace the auto_learn.rs prune loop: since SQLite doesn't allow binding table names, enumerate the tables in match arms, each calling a pre-written parameterized DELETE with a bound LIMIT.
